codeReview.summary: The fix replaces `replaceSearchChunks(...)` with `applySearchChunkDiff(...)` and introduces diff classification, but it still does not satisfy the failure's core requirement for conversation reprojections: unchanged chunks are not skipped at persistence/index level when source versions change. Because chunk IDs are source-version-coupled, small edits/metadata updates rekey chunk IDs for unchanged content, and the diff path deletes/inserts those chunks anyway.

issues.description: Chunk identity remains coupled to `sourceVersionID` (`chunkID(...)` payload includes `sourceVersionID`), while `conversationSourceVersionID` is derived from full conversation metadata/content (`conversationContentHash`, lines 48-70). Any conversation source-version change therefore rekeys unchanged chunks, preventing true unchanged-skip behavior for typical small edits.

In `applyChunkDiff`, unchanged-content hashes with rekeyed IDs are handled by deleting `oldIDs.subtracting(newIDs)` and inserting new IDs (line 418). With source-version-coupled chunk IDs, this becomes delete+insert churn for unchanged content, so projection still rewrites effectively the full chunk set instead of only impacted chunks.

The new pipeline-level metadata-change test explicitly expects chunk IDs to change (`XCTAssertNotEqual(initialChunkIDs, finalChunkIDs)`) and validates hash/embedding continuity, but it does not assert store-level write-amplification bounds. This allows full rekey rewrites of unchanged chunks to pass while the feature contract calls for unchanged persistence skips.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission feature expectations and architecture constraints are misaligned for chunk persistence semantics.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature contract requires unchanged chunk skips at store/index level (`features.json`: lines 773, 780-783), but architecture explicitly documents source-version-coupled chunk IDs (`.factory/library/architecture.md`: lines 70-73), which rekeys chunks on source-version changes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The indexing-efficiency skill procedure includes reading `user-testing.md`, but this step is not evidenced in the reviewed transcript skeleton despite `followedProcedure: true` in handoff feedback.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill requirement in `.factory/skills/indexing-efficiency-worker/SKILL.md` step 1; transcript skeleton shows reads for `mission.md`, `AGENTS.md`, and `architecture.md`, but no `user-testing.md` read for session `4d6db6a8-0520-4a6c-8651-9d4203b57246`.

summary: Fail: the fix removes direct replace-all calls but does not fully resolve the original failure. Under current chunk identity semantics, unchanged conversation chunks are still rekeyed and rewritten during reprojecting source-version changes, so true persistence-level unchanged-skip behavior remains unmet.
